trivia_bullets.text: Theros Beyond Death (THB) released 2020-01-24 and returns to the Greek-mythology-inspired plane of Theros; the set's mechanical throughline is the Escape keyword, letting cards recur from the graveyard. Archons are divine constructs of white mana that enforce order on behalf of the gods [Scryfall].

"The archon fell like a star from the sky, meeting the rising sun at the horizon's edge." — The Cosmogony [Scryfall]. The Cosmogony is a fictional Theran sacred text describing the creation of the cosmos; its appearance on multiple THB cards frames the set's mythology of death and return.

When Archon of Falling Stars dies, it may return any enchantment from your graveyard to the battlefield — including Auras, which bypass targeting and can attach to anything you choose. This makes it a payoff for white enchantment-graveyard loops in Pioneer and Commander. EDHREC rank 20559 indicates niche Commander play [Scryfall, EDHREC].

Archon of Falling Stars was reprinted in Foundations Jumpstart (2024), the only paper reprint outside THB promos, confirming Wizards considers the enchantment-recursion trigger a durable teaching tool for new players [Scryfall prints].
